mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns in >55% of periods following a rate increase, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive mean 60-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained economic output and weakening corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in >50% of cases, or the predictive IC reverses sign.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.4%, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with 20-day forward returns, or the mean return following positive GDP surprises is negative in >50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below its 6-month trend signals a structural supply-side contraction, reducing economic capacity and driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of periods where INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average, or the signal Rank IC is negative.
